title: Feature request: Variables of data type real in initial construct
body:
Verilog 2005 specifies the variable data types integer, real, realtime, reg, and time, of which Yosys seems to have full support for integer, and partial support for real (parameter real).

It would be very useful if the support for real were to be extended to initial constructs, i.e.

```
initial begin
    real r = 10.0;
    ...
end
```

This currently fails with the error "ERROR: syntax error, unexpected TOK_REAL".

Allowing real in this setting would facilitate parametrization of modules which depend on iterative floating point calculations, e.g. to initialize BRAM. It also seems like a logical enhancement, since Yosys already supports real math functions, i.e. $sin(x) et al.
